questions:
🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Save your bacon fat in a small jar — it is the best base for next morning's cowboy coffee and adds serious flavor to any bean dish.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Use a cast iron skillet with a lid — it holds heat better than foil and cooks evenly over coals. Great for breakfast, lunch, or dinner.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

No-Cook Summer Camp Snacks

When it is hot and you do not want to fire up the stove: trail mix, jerky, hard cheese, fresh fruit, and nut butter packets.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Ditch the paper towels at camp — a plain old cotton bandana does the job better and generates zero trash.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Bring a zip-lock bag of dry seasonings pre-mixed. One bag = taco night, another = breakfast scramble seasoning. Saves space and your back.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Frozen water bottles do double duty — cold drinking water AND keeping your cooler temps stable for days.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

The best way to clean a cast iron at camp? Hot water, a scrubby stick, then immediately dry and oil. Never soap it after cooking.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Pre-slice your onions and peppers at home and store in a paper towel-lined ziploc. They will keep for 3 days at camp and you will not cry in the wilderness.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Cowboy coffee tip: Add a splash of cold water at the end to settle the grounds. No filter needed.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Hang your cooler from a high tree branch at night in bear country — it is not the food, it is the smell.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

Foil pack meals are great but hard to manage on wind. This tip: use a cast iron skillet as a lid over foil and it will not blow away.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

If you forget your coffee filter, a clean bandana works in a pinch. Just make sure it is rinsed well.

🔥 Camp Cook Tip

A small squirt bottle of dish soap plus water solves everything when you need to deglaze a hot pan safely at camp.
